decisions.title: Migrate from Amplitude to native telemetry infrastructure
decisions.context: 4 active tickets addressing Amplitude reliability issues (ENG-9611, 9610, 9609, 9440). High-cardinality events causing ingestion problems. Cluster of tracking-related work suggests systemic fragility. Decision: Build internal telemetry vs. fix Amplitude integration.

arguments.for: Native telemetry aligns with 'Build the Moat' pillar. Eliminates third-party dependency. Enables better product usage tracking for North Star metric (Decisions Captured). Current Amplitude issues blocking product insights.
arguments.against: Significant engineering investment. Amplitude provides mature analytics UI. Team bandwidth constrained with Explore V2, Canvas, Edge work. Risk of building another system to maintain.

decisions.title: Prioritize Sift Edge production readiness over feature expansion
decisions.context: ENG-9558 (test coverage), ENG-9603 (GHA workflows/protections), ENG-9456 (CSRS porting) indicate Edge is moving toward production but lacks operational maturity. Competing with E2 stability work (ENG-9608 annotations, ENG-9606 search bugs, ENG-9601 linking issues).
decisions.blockedBy: Need Edge customer deployment timeline and revenue impact assessment

arguments.for: Edge is strategic moat-building product. Production readiness enables customer deployments and validates embedded strategy ('Embed Deeper'). Current work suggests near-term launch window.
arguments.against: E2/Explore V2 stability issues (5+ open bugs) risk existing customer satisfaction. 'Nail the Core' pillar suggests fixing foundation first. Edge sync issues could compound with production load.

decisions.title: Consolidate authentication infrastructure (Keycloak vs alternatives)
decisions.context: ENG-9546 (ephemeral cert disabling), ENG-9604 (automate org user creation), ENG-9543 (AWS credentials break-glass) suggest auth complexity. Multiple authentication touchpoints across products.

arguments.for: Simplified auth reduces operational overhead. Improves security posture. Enables faster customer onboarding (supports 'Embed Deeper'). Break-glass procedures indicate current fragility.
arguments.against: Auth migration is high-risk. Keycloak may be sufficient with better documentation/automation. Limited signals suggesting customer-facing auth issues.

decisions.title: Establish frontend code quality gates before expanding UI surface area
decisions.context: DES-151 (context menu audit), ENG-9420 (apply default UX), ENG-9379 (overflow on rules page), ENG-9373 (overflow on edit config) show accumulating UI polish debt. Canvas and Explore V2 will add significant UI complexity.

arguments.for: Prevents compounding UI debt as Canvas/Explore V2 ship. Design audit (DES-151) suggests systematic review underway. Quality gates support 'Nail the Core' by ensuring consistent UX.
arguments.against: May slow Canvas/Explore V2 velocity. Overflow issues appear cosmetic vs. functional. Customer feedback not indicating major UX concerns in signals.

decisions.title: Define data partitioning strategy for multi-tenant scale
decisions.context: ENG-9504 (sift_app partitioning by orgId), ENG-9423 (multi-channel parquet support) indicate movement toward better data isolation. Critical for 'Embed Deeper' as customer data volumes grow.

arguments.for: Proper partitioning enables customer-level performance isolation. Supports enterprise sales motion. Reduces blast radius of issues. Necessary foundation for Ask SIFT at scale.
arguments.against: Migration risk to existing customer data. May require downtime or gradual rollout. Premature optimization if current partitioning adequate for 2026 customer volume.
